title: The Demon Lord
body: Rimuru Tempest ｢リムル＝テンペスト, rimuru tenpesuto｣ is the main protagonist of That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime and the reincarnation of Satoru Mikami.

title: Forger Family
body:
The family consists of:

Loid Forger: The adoptive father of Anya, who works as a psychiatrist at Berlint General Hospital, while he secretly works as a spy for WISE.
Yor Forger: The mother of the family. She works as a civil servant at Berlint City Hall while moonlighting for an unknown assassin organization known as Garden by the code name "Thorn Princess." Yor thinks Anya is Loid's biological daughter.
Anya Forger: The adopted daughter of Loid and Yor Forger. She was a research experiment used by a certain organization and gained the ability to read minds. After escaping their facility, Anya moved from orphanages multiple times until she met Loid, who adopted her.
Bond Forger: The pet of the family. Similar to Anya, he was used as a military research test subject funded by the previous Ostanian government. After the research project was shut down, Bond found his way into the black market, where he was sold to be a bomb dog. From there, he would eventually meet Anya due to his precognition ability given to him by the experiments.

title: Caped Baldy
body:
Saitama (サイタマ, Saitama) is the main protagonist of the series and the titular One-Punch Man. He stands as the most powerful being in the narrative. Saitama faces a self-imposed existential crisis, as he is now too powerful to gain any thrill from battle.[7]

Initially just a hero for fun,[8] Saitama later decides to register as a professional hero with the Hero Association[9] after realizing that nobody recognizes him as a hero. He takes on the responsibility of protecting his home in Z-City from Mysterious Beings, villains, and various threats.[10]

Unknown to most characters, Saitama is the rumored The Ghost Town Monster (ゴーストタウンの化物, Gōsuto Taun no Bakemono) in Z-City Ghost Town, something that many believe resides there.[11][12] After the incident involving the Monster Association and the subsequent destruction of his apartment, he now lives in Hero Apartment 104 at the Hero Association Headquarters in A-City. Within the Hero Association, he is assigned the hero name Caped Baldy (ハゲマント, Hagemanto; Viz: Bald Cape)[13] and currently holds A-Class Rank 39.

title: Titan's Shinigami
body: Levi Ackerman (リヴァイ・アッカーマン Rivai Akkāman?), often formally referred to as Captain Levi (リヴァイ兵長 Rivai Heichō?), is the squad captain (兵士長 Heishichō?, lit. "leader of the soldiers") of the Special Operations Squad within the Survey Corps and is widely known as humanity's strongest soldier.[1]

title: King of Frauds
body:
Ryomen Sukuna (両りょう面めん宿すく儺な Ryōmen Sukuna?), more often referred to as simply Sukuna (宿すく儺な Sukuna?), is history’s strongest jujutsu sorcerer, born over a thousand years ago. Regarded as the undisputed King of Curses (呪のろいの王おう Noroi no Ō?), Sukuna is one of the primary antagonists of the Jujutsu Kaisen series.

According to legend, Sukuna was an Imaginary Demon (仮か想そうの鬼き神じん Kasō no Kijin?) during the Heian Era, the Golden Age of Jujutsu. In truth, he was an undefeated human sorcerer also known as the Disgraced One (堕だ天てん Daten?),[2] having felled countless jujutsu sorcerers that challenged him. After accepting a proposal from Kenjaku to participate in the Culling Game,[3] Sukuna continued existing after death by transforming himself into a cursed object, a state where his cursed energy and soul was split into his twenty indestructible fingers.[1]

Sukuna was incarnated in June 2018 when Yuji Itadori consumed one of his fingers.[4] Sukuna was trapped inside Yuji's body and was rarely in control until he managed to transfer his soul into the body of Megumi Fushiguro.[5]

title: The Blue Eyed Monster
body: Satoru Gojo (五ご条じょう悟さとる Gojō Satoru?) is one of the main protagonists of the Jujutsu Kaisen series. He is a special grade jujutsu sorcerer and widely recognized as the strongest in the world. Satoru is the pride of the Gojo Clan, the first person to inherit both the Limitless and the Six Eyes in four hundred years. He works as a teacher at the Tokyo Jujutsu High and uses his influence to protect and train strong young allies.

title: Killer of the Kin
body: Itachi Uchiha (うちはイタチ, Uchiha Itachi) was a shinobi of Konohagakure's Uchiha clan who served as an Anbu Captain. He later became an international criminal after murdering his entire clan, sparing only his younger brother, Sasuke. He afterwards joined the international criminal organisation known as Akatsuki, whose activity brought him into frequent conflict with Konoha and its ninja — including Sasuke who sought to avenge their clan by killing Itachi. Following his death, Itachi's motives were revealed to be more complicated than they seemed and that his actions were only ever in the interest of his brother and village, making him remain a loyal shinobi of Konohagakure to the very end.

title: Ghost of Uchiha
body: Madara Uchiha (うちはマダラ, Uchiha Madara) was the legendary leader of the Uchiha Clan and a reincarnation of Indra Ōtsutsuki. He founded Konohagakure alongside his childhood friend and rival, Hashirama Senju, with the intention of bringing about an era of peace. When the two couldn't agree on how to achieve that peace, they fought for control of the village, a conflict which ended in Madara's death. Madara, however, rewrote his death and went into hiding to work on his own plans. Unable to complete it in his natural life, he entrusted his knowledge and plans to Obito Uchiha shortly before his actual death. Years later, Madara would be revived during the Fourth Shinobi World War with his goal involving the Infinite Tsukuyomi having reached fruition, only to be upended by Black Zetsu and its own ancient machinations. Finally, realising the error of his ways, he made amends with Hashirama before his final death.

title: UltraMan
body:
Ultraman (ウルトラマン, Urutoraman) is an alien from a place called the Land of Light in Nebula M78, who chased the monster Bemular to Planet Earth, and merged with Shin Hayata during his tenure there. He is the first Ultra to visit Earth and defended the planet against Kaiju until he was recalled after his battle with Zetton. Ultraman was given his title by his human host.
It was the introduction of this iconic character that had popularized the Ultraman Series in Japan and the world, arguably being the face of the franchise as a whole.

Subtitle: Monster Disposal Specialist (怪獣退治の専門家, Kaijū Taiji no Senmonka)[1]

title: Ryukendo
body: Madan Senki Ryukendo (魔弾戦記リュウケンドー Madan Senki Ryūkendō?) is a Japanese tokusatsu superhero, action and science fiction television series. The program aired at 7:00 JST on TV Aichi from January 8 to December 31, 2006, with each episode lasted around 24 to 25 minutes. It is created by Ōji Hiroi, Yūichirō Hira, Yuzuru Miyauchi and Tsuyoshi Yoshida, with Tōru Kuroda and Hiroshi Takegawa as executive producers as well as Hitoshi Nakajima and Yūto Suzuki as producers.

title: BEN 10
body: Benjamin Kirby "Ben" Tennyson[37][pop-up 2][DVD 1] is the titular main protagonist of the Ben 10 franchise. He was an ordinary 10-year-old boy until he found the Omnitrix, a powerful watch-like device that allowed him to turn into 10 different aliens. Though initially immature and clumsy, Ben grew to be a true hero. By the time he was 16, he had become famous in not only his hometown of Bellwood but on Earth and beyond. Ben has also been deputized by the Plumbers and has gained more aliens as time progressed.

title: Power Rangers S.P.D.
body:
Power Rangers S.P.D. (Space Patrol Delta) is the thirteenth season of the Power Rangers television series, a science fantasy, action-adventure, and police procedural show that originally aired in 2005. The series is set in the year 2025, when humans and extraterrestrial beings live together on Earth. 
Synopsis
In 2025, the Earth has opened its doors to aliens, but the peace is threatened by the planet-conquering Troobian Empire, led by Emperor Gruumm. The primary law enforcement agency is the S.P.D., which trains cadets to become Power Rangers. When the elite A-Squad mysteriously disappears, the B-Squad team is promoted to defend the planet. 
The B-Squad initially consists of Sky Tate (Blue Ranger), Bridge Carson (Green Ranger), and Sydney Drew (Pink Ranger). They are forced to team up with two reformed street thieves, Jack Landors and Z Delgado, who become the Red and Yellow Rangers, respectively. The series focuses on their journey to overcome personal differences, learn teamwork, and eventually defeat the Troobian Empire with the help of their canine alien commander, Anubis "Doggie" Cruger, who also joins the fight as the Shadow Ranger. 
Main Characters and Cast
The B-Squad Rangers and main supporting characters include:
Jack Landors (Brandon Jay McLaren) - S.P.D. Red Ranger
Sky Tate (Chris Violette) - S.P.D. Blue Ranger (later Red Ranger)
Bridge Carson (Matt Austin) - S.P.D. Green Ranger (later Blue and Red Ranger)
Z Delgado (Monica May) - S.P.D. Yellow Ranger
Sydney Drew (Alycia Purrott) - S.P.D. Pink Ranger
Anubis "Doggie" Cruger (voiced by John Tui) - S.P.D. Shadow Ranger and Commander
Sam (voiced by Brett Stewart) - S.P.D. Omega Ranger
Dr. Katherine "Kat" Manx (Michelle Langstone) - S.P.D. chief technologist and temporary Kat Ranger

title: Why DAN DA DAN is the Anime of the Year (So Far)
body:
The story follows two high schoolers who are total opposites:
Momo Ayase: A popular girl who believes in ghosts but thinks aliens are fake.
Okarun (Ken Takakura): An occult nerd who believes in aliens but thinks ghosts are fake.
To prove the other wrong, they send each other to famous "haunted" and "UFO" spots. Plot Twist: They are both right. Aliens are real, ghosts are real, and they are both incredibly dangerous. What follows is a chaotic battle for survival, stolen "family jewels" (literally), and a blooming romance amidst the madness

title: The worlds of Poket Monsters
body:
# Do you remember the first time you walked into the long grass?

For me, it was the pixelated monochrome of Pallet Town on a Game Boy Color. For you, it might have been the lush 3D wilds of the Galar region, or perhaps the open plains of Hisui. But regardless of when you started, we all share that singular, electric feeling: the possibility that the next encounter could be the one.

As we look back on over 25 years of this franchise, I’ve been thinking about what actually keeps us coming back. Is it really about **"catching them all"**? I don’t think so. In fact, I think the magic lies in never truly finishing the journey.

The Comfort of the Loop
On the surface, *Pokémon *is a simple JRPG loop: Catch, Train, Battle, Repeat.

But there is a rhythmic comfort in that loop that few other games replicate. In a world that is often chaotic and unpredictable, the Pokémon world offers rules we understand. Types have strengths and weaknesses. Effort Values (EVs) yield results. If you put in the time to raise that Magikarp, you will get a Gyarados.

This direct correlation between effort and reward is satisfying in a way real life rarely is. It is a "cozy" grind—one we are happy to repeat, generation after generation.

More Than Just Data
If Pokémon were just numbers on a screen, we would have quit years ago. The genius of Game Freak’s design is in the personality.

The Stories We Tell: Nuzlocke challenges prove that we crave narrative. We don't just want a Charizard; we want our Charizard that survived a crit-hit Hydro Pump against the Elite Four on 1 HP.

The Shiny Hunt: Why do we spend hours resetting games for a different colored sprite? Because we value rarity and personal investment over utility.

The Evolution of Community
The biggest shift in recent years hasn't been the graphics (though Scarlet and Violet certainly sparked debates there); it’s been the community.

We used to trade via link cables in schoolyards. Now, we coordinate Tera Raids on Discord, trade breedjects via Wonder Trade, and watch VGC championships on Twitch. The game has evolved from a solitary adventure into a global ecosystem. Even if you stop playing the main games, you're likely still engaging—whether it's through the TCG, Pokémon GO, or simply arguing about which Eeveelution is the best (it’s Umbreon, by the way).

Conclusion
We never really "Catch 'Em All" because the goalpost always moves—and that's a good thing. The Pokédex expands, new regions are discovered, and new mechanics are introduced.

We don't come back to finish the job. We come back because, for a little while, we get to be 10 years old again, stepping into the tall grass, wondering what we’ll find.

What was your first starter Pokémon? Let me know in the comments below!

title: The Girl who survived the anomally of the created fetous sprit Kabuto
body: Yayoi Hozuki (寶月 夜宵, Hōzuki Yayoi) is the main protagonist of the Dark Gathering series. She is a young girl with a spiritual constitution who's collecting dangerous ghost in order to get enough power to rescue her mother's spirit from an extremely Evil Ghost, Kuubou.
